subject: Physics
title: Sonoluminescence: Sound Creates Light
description: A 60-second lesson on Sonoluminescence: Sound Creates Light.

hook.text: Blast sound waves into water and a tiny trapped bubble can glow with light. Sound literally creates light.

buildup.text: Intense sound waves make a bubble rapidly expand and collapse thousands of times per second.

discovery.text: During collapse, the gas inside reaches temperatures exceeding 12,000°C—hotter than the Sun's surface.

twist.text: Nobody fully understands why it gets so hot. Some theories suggest the temperature may reach millions of degrees.

climax.text: Sonoluminescence remains one of the most puzzling phenomena in physics—sound defying expectations.

punchline.text: Sound makes a bubble glow. Nobody fully knows why. Beautiful mystery.

quiz.question: What is sonoluminescence?
options: Light produced by the collapse of a sound-driven bubble in liquid
A technique for recording sound using light
